mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) over three months indicates expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate earnings growth and boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO stood at 100.3 in December 2009, up from trough of 98.5 in June 2009
failure_condition: If INDPRO declines for two consecutive months but S&P 500 still posts positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of such periods, or if the Spearman rank correlation between INDPRO and forward returns is below 0.1 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 9.5% after being above 9.5% for at least two months signals that the labor market has entered a sustained recovery, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which drives positive equity returns over the following 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops below 9.5% after being above 9.5% for at least 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 10.0% in December 2009, consistent with high unemployment
failure_condition: If after the trigger fires, S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, or if the average return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound and inflation measures (CPIAUCSL) begin to rise moderately, it indicates that reflationary monetary policy is gaining traction, which reduces deflation risk and supports higher equity prices over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12%, CPIAUCSL at 217.5 in December 2009
failure_condition: If in any rolling 1-year period, when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth is between 0.1% and 0.3%, the S&P 500 60-day return is negative more often than positive (i.e., median return < 0).

mechanism_narrative: In the early expansion phase following a deep recession, a quarterly GDP growth rate above 2% annualized signals that the recovery is self-sustaining, which reduces recession risk and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 12973.0 in Q3 2009, down from peak of 13361.6 in Q4 2007
failure_condition: If after a GDP growth > 2% trigger, the S&P 500 60-day return is negative in more than 40% of occurrences, or if the average return is not significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days as the market absorbs the data.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 352985 in December 2009, up from 344573 in September 2009
failure_condition: If over a 2-year rolling window, the correlation between month-over-month change in RSAFS and subsequent 5-day SPX return is negative or zero, or if the mean return following RSAFS increases is not higher than following decreases.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above 600,000 units annually after prolonged weakness signals a turning point in the housing market, which is a leading indicator for broader economic activity and supports positive equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above 600 after being below 600 for at least 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 574 in November 2009, still below 600
failure_condition: If following the trigger, the median 60-day SPX return is not positive, or if the trigger occurs and SPX falls more than 5% within 60 days in more than 20% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous improvement in both employment (nonfarm payrolls rising) and unemployment (rate falling) provides a comprehensive signal of labor market healing, which boosts aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 130.9 million in December 2009, UNRATE 10.0%
failure_condition: If in any 1-year window, when both PAYEMS increases month-over-month and UNRATE decreases month-over-month, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative more than 40% of the time, or if the average return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary regime where month-over-month CPI growth is below 0.1%, further declines in inflation indicate weakening aggregate demand, which depresses corporate pricing power and earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL MoM growth in December 2009 was 0.1% (217.541 vs 217.3 in November)
failure_condition: If after the trigger, S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances, or if the average return is significantly above zero (t-test p < 0.05).
